doc_title: US EPA: OSA: Validation and Peer Review of U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Chemical Methods of Analysis

steps.text: Peer review the chemical method of analysis in accordance with the EPA Peer Review Handbook or a documented alternate procedure.
Initiate an intralaboratory method validation study.
Initiate an interlaboratory method validation study.
Clearly describe the measurement process components subjected to validation.
Clearly define the purpose of the method and the intended use of the data.
Define and clearly describe the method scope and applicability.
Perform proper sample preservation and storage stability studies during method development.
Evaluate selectivity by testing appropriate matrix blanks and fortified matrix blanks.
Demonstrate the characteristics of the calibration function and justify the selected calibration model.
Evaluate trueness at the extremes of the quantitation range and at regulatory levels.
Evaluate method repeatability and reproducibility at the extremes of the quantitation range and at regulatory levels.
Define the detection limit and verify limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models.
Conduct ruggedness testing to examine method performance under minor variations in operating conditions.
Prepare a method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.
Publish the EPA method of analysis.

preconditions.text: Ensure a detailed procedure has been written, operational parameters are optimized and finalized, and no additional changes are anticipated.
Ensure proper sample preservation and storage stability studies were performed.
Ensure a successful intralaboratory study has been completed.
Ensure ruggedness testing is completed and critical control points are identified in the documented method.
Ensure the issuing entity has demonstrated a pressing programmatic need for publishing a national method.
Ensure the interlaboratory method validation study is completed.
Ensure the method has been peer reviewed or reviewed by a documented alternate procedure.

constraints.text: If procedural changes are made during the intralaboratory study that may affect performance characteristics, some or all performance tests may need to be repeated.
If procedural changes are required during the interlaboratory study, some or all aspects of the interlaboratory study may need to be repeated.
For emergency response situations, follow the emergency response method validation policy (Directive FEM-2010-01).
